fields.helpText: This is the site for which the Accounts Receivable activity will be tracked.  A station can only have one site defined and multiple divisional sites are not supported.                                   This identifies charges or events to a site in the event that information is shared or sent to a centralized system.
Determines if auto-posting of medical claims is enabled for this site.
Determines if a medical claim amount can be auto-decreased for claims with non-zero payments during the auto-post processing.
This parameter determines the default number of days to wait before  applying the auto-decrease adjustment to non-zero payments being processed by the auto-post processing.

This parameter allows sites to specify the claim dollar amount maximum of  an automatic decrease adjustment that is made for a third party medical claims, if auto-posting and auto-decrease of third party medical claims is turned on for the site. This parameter will default to null and require a response from 1 to 99999 dollars for the parameter question to allow sites to specify the claim dollar amount maximum of an automatic decrease adjustment that is made for third party medical claims.

The number of calendar days beyond which un-posted medical payments  (EFTs) will prevent the user from posting newer medical EFTs without  posting the older payments first.   A value of 21 is the initial default.  The user can reset the value to a  number from 14 to 60, inclusive, but cannot delete the value.

The number of calendar days beyond which unposted pharmacy payments  (EFTs) will prevent the user from posting newer pharmacy EFTs without  posting the older payments first.   The user can reset the value to a number between 21 and 99, inclusive, but the user cannot delete the value.
This field is the date/time the RCDPE AUTO-PAY EXCLUSION file (#344.6) was updated for new payers by the Accounts Receivable Nightly Process.

Used in posting prevention when working on the ERA Worklist.  Unposted  EFTs that are at least two months older than this date are not considered  when determining if there are aged, unposted EFTs on the system.   Do not edit this date.  It is set at package installation and must not be  changed.
This field determines on which day of the week that workload notification  bulletins are to be sent to the RCDPE AUDIT mail group.
Determines if a medical claim amount can be auto-decreased for claims with no payments (zero dollar) that are not reversals during the auto-post processing.

This parameter determines the default number of days to wait before applying the auto-decrease adjustment to medical claims with no payments on denial ERAs being processed by the auto-post processing.

The number of calendar days beyond which un-posted Tricare  payments (EFTs) will prevent the user from posting newer  medical EFTs without posting the older payments first.                 A value of 30 is the initial default.  The user can set the value to a number from 14 to 60, inclusive, but cannot delete the value.
Determines if auto-posting of pharmacy claims is enabled for this site.
Determines if auto-decrease of pharmacy claims is enabled for this site.  NOTE:  Not editable and set to Disabled until further notice

The history multiple contains a log of changes to EDI Lockbox auto-post and auto-decrease parameters.  The date and time of the change, the user who made the change and the old and new values of the parameter are stored.  This information is used to print the Auto Parameter History Report on demand.
An override will allow unrestricted scratchpad creation within the ERA  Worklist option for one day.
An override will allow unrestricted scratchpad creation within the ERA  Worklist option for one day.
A record of the person who entered the medical override is needed for auditing purposes.
A record of the person who entered the pharmacy override is needed for auditing purposes.
The reason for entering the Medical Override must be recorded for auditing purposes.
The reason for entering the Pharmacy Override must be recorded for auditing purposes.
An override will allow unrestricted scratchpad creation within  the ERA Worklist option for one day.
A record of the person who entered the Tricare override is needed for auditing purposes.
The reason for entering the Tricare override must be recorded for auditing purposes.
